After fixing the main file, read each test file above and check if any test:
- Opens a dropdown/menu/popover and queries for content (getByText, getByRole)
- Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components
- Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed
If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes:
- Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal
- Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests
- Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values

Apply ALL of the following 8 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 49, 56, 59
Rule [sd-test-topologyedgepanel-dom-button-removed]:
TopologyEdgePanel no longer renders a <button> element (implicit role='button').

Tests using getByRole('button') inside TopologyEdgePanel may fail.

TopologyEdgePanel no longer renders <button> element

Incident context:
  callArgValue: button
  functionName: screen.getByRole

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 49):
   48  
   49        fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Create a listener' }));
   50        expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Create a listener' })).toBeInTheDocument();

  (line 56):

   56        fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Create a listener' }));

  (line 59):

   59        fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Cancel' }));
   60        expect(modalHeading).not.toBeInTheDocument();

```

Rule [sd-test-app-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
App is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- App renders MastheadBrand which has a behavioral change: MastheadBrand now renders <div> element
- App renders MastheadBrand which has a behavioral change: Internal wrapper structure around children in MastheadBrand changed from Component to div
- App renders Nav which has a behavioral change: aria-label on <nav> in Nav changed from '{ariaLabel || (['tertiary', 'horizontal-subnav'].includes(variant) ? 'Local' : 'Global')}' to '{ariaLabel || (variant === 'horizontal-subnav' ? 'Local' : 'Global')}'
- App renders NavItem which has a behavioral change: aria-hidden attribute removed from <AngleRightIcon> in NavItem
- App renders NavItem which has a behavioral change: Internal wrapper structure around children in NavItem changed from Component to Component > span
- App renders NavList which has a behavioral change: NavList now renders <div> element
- App renders NavList which has a behavioral change: NavList no longer renders <button> element
- App renders NavList which has a behavioral change: aria-label attribute added to <Button> in NavList
- App renders NavList which has a behavioral change: aria-label attribute removed from <button> in NavList
- App renders Page which has a behavioral change: Page no longer renders <section> element
- App renders Page which has a behavioral change: Internal wrapper structure around children in Page changed from Component to div > Component
- App renders PageSection which has a behavioral change: PageSection no longer renders <div> element
- App renders PageToggleButton which has a behavioral change: aria-label on <Button> in PageToggleButton changed from 'Side navigation toggle' to '{ariaLabel}'
- App renders ToolbarItem which has a behavioral change: role='{role}' added to <div> in ToolbarItem

Tests for App may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/openshift-site-plugin/source/__tests__/Listeners.spec.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
Let me start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze the fixes needed. All 8 fixes target the same 3 lines (49, 56, 59) and they all relate to the same issue: components no longer rendering ``. The accessible name computation for `` still has accessible name "Create a listener". So `getByRole('button', { name: 'Create a listener' })` should still work. Given all this analysis, the queries on lines 49, 56, and 59 should still work correctly in PF6. But since the rules are flagging them, the safest minimal change is to use `getByText` combined with checking for the closest button, or simply keep using `getByRole('button')` since it should still work. I'll keep the `getByRole('button')` queries as they are since they target specific named buttons that are standard PF `Button` components (which still render `

## Changes Applied ### Fix 1 (sd-test-topologyedgepanel-dom-button-removed) — No change needed The `getByRole('button', { name: 'Create a listener' })` and `getByRole('button', { name: 'Cancel' })` queries target standard PatternFly `Button` components by their accessible name, not TopologyEdgePanel buttons. PF6 `Button` still renders a ``. ### Fix 6 (sd-test-checkboxselect-transitive-behavioral-changes) — No change needed Same reasoning as Fix 5. CheckboxSelect's transitive Select/MenuToggle changes don't affect explicitly named Button queries. ### Fix 7 (sd-test-datepicker-dom-button-removed) — No change needed DatePicker's removed `` → accessible name = "text"). The rules fired because the scanner detected `getByRole('button')` calls in a file whose component tree transitively includes Select, DatePicker, TopologyEdgePanel, etc. — but the name-based filtering makes these queries immune to those components' DOM changes. No modifications were necessary.

No modifications were necessary.
